differential_diagnoses: Metastatic carcinoma (e.g., HPV-positive oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma)
next_steps: Secure the airway due to potential for rapid expansion or hemorrhage
Excisional biopsy under general anesthesia with readiness for significant hemorrhage management
Histopathological examination with immunohistochemistry (Desmin, MyoD1, myogenin, pan-CK, CD20, CD3)
Molecular testing including FISH for FOXO1 translocation and NGS for rhabdomyosarcoma-associated mutations
Contrast-enhanced MRI of the head and neck for better soft-tissue characterization
Staging PET-CT to evaluate for systemic disease or distant involvement

differential_diagnoses: Pseudoaneurysm of the internal carotid or ascending pharyngeal artery
Vascular neoplasm (e.g., Angiosarcoma or Kaposi sarcoma)
Pleomorphic adenoma of minor salivary gland with secondary hemorrhage
next_steps: Computed tomography angiography (CTA) or magnetic resonance angiography (MRA) of the neck to definitively rule out a vascular lesion or pseudoaneurysm before any further biopsy
Interventional radiology consultation for potential pre-biopsy embolization if a vascular pathology is identified
Excisional biopsy under general anesthesia with airway protection and preparation for massive hemorrhage
Histopathological evaluation with immunohistochemistry (Desmin, MyoD1, myogenin, CD20, CD3, pan-cytokeratin, HHV-8)
Molecular testing including FISH for FOXO1 translocation and NGS for rhabdomyosarcoma-associated mutations
Staging PET-CT to evaluate for systemic disease involvement
